title: Light Flaky Slovakian Dough Dumplings
description: A traditional Slovakian dish featuring fermented dough and a unique blend of flavors, including onion salt and yuzu-cure, this recipe is shojin-friendly and perfect for a quick fix, the dumplings are made with a flaky dough that is fermented to give it a light and airy texture
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, yeast, onion salt, yuzu-cure, and salt.
Gradually add the warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat a large skillet or frying pan with the vegetable oil over medium heat.
Once the oil is hot, add the fermented dough to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until it is golden brown and cooked through.
Remove the dumplings from the skillet and serve hot.
Traditionally, these dumplings are served with a side of steamed vegetables or a salad.
To make the dish more substantial, you can add some sauteed mushrooms or tofu to the dumplings.
